metadata.note: Pan-PPAR agonists activate all three PPAR subtypes (α, β/δ, γ) for metabolic diseases (NASH/MASH, diabetes, dyslipidemia)

chemical_identifiers.notes: Pan-PPAR agonist designed to activate all three PPAR subtypes (α, β/δ, γ) with balanced potency.

clinical_trials.note: Phase III trial for NASH/MASH with liver fibrosis. Promising results in improving both steatohepatitis and fibrosis scores.

compounds.key_findings: Most advanced pan-PPAR agonist in development. Specifically designed to activate all three PPAR subtypes (α, β/δ, γ) with balanced potency. Phase III NATIVE trial for NASH/MASH has shown promising results in improving both steatohepatitis and fibrosis scores. Represents the most promising candidate for future global approval in the NASH/MASH indication.
compounds.notes: Phase III. NATIVE trial for NASH/MASH. Most promising pan-PPAR candidate.

chemical_identifiers.notes: First pan-PPAR agonist specifically approved for type 2 diabetes treatment in China. Demonstrates balanced activation across all three PPAR subtypes.

compounds.key_findings: First pan-PPAR agonist approved for type 2 diabetes (China, October 2021). Brand name: Bilessglu. Represents a significant milestone as the first pan-PPAR agonist specifically approved for T2DM treatment. Demonstrates balanced activation across all three PPAR subtypes (α, β/δ, γ). Only approved in China as of 2026.
compounds.notes: Approved China Oct 2021. Brand: Bilessglu. First pan-PPAR for T2DM.

chemical_identifiers.notes: Historically classified as a fibrate (primarily PPARα), but has demonstrated pan-PPAR activity in multiple studies. In clinical use for over 45 years.

compounds.key_findings: Legacy pan-PPAR agonist approved globally since 1978. Brand name: Bezalip. Historically classified as a fibrate (primarily PPARα), but multiple studies have demonstrated pan-PPAR activity (α, β/δ, γ). In clinical use for over 45 years for hyperlipidemia and metabolic syndrome. Pan-PPAR activity was recognized retrospectively.
compounds.notes: Approved globally since 1978. Brand: Bezalip. Pan-PPAR activity recognized retrospectively.
